name: American Consumer Credit Counseling, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling agency offering free consultations, debt management programs, and bankruptcy counseling to help consumers reduce interest rates and regain financial stability.
description_long:
American Consumer Credit Counseling, Inc. (ACCC) is a non-profit credit counseling agency reachable at 800-769-3571. The organization serves individuals and families struggling with credit card debt, offering a structured path toward financial recovery through counseling, debt management, and educational programs. ACCC holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and reports thousands of satisfied clients who have completed its programs.

ACCC's core offering is its Debt Management Program (DMP), through which counselors negotiate with creditors to significantly reduce interest rates and monthly payments, helping clients pay off debt faster than on their own. Beyond debt management, the agency provides pre- and post-bankruptcy counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, first-time homebuyer courses, and student loan help — a breadth of service that suggests HUD-approved status. All services begin with a free consultation.

Several features distinguish ACCC from for-profit debt relief companies. The agency explicitly states it does not loan money, removing any risk of predatory lending. It offers a dedicated client portal (CreditU App and web login) where enrolled clients can track program details, due dates, and documents. The free initial consultation and compassionate counseling approach position ACCC as a low-pressure entry point for consumers who are unsure where to start.

ACCC is a solid, low-risk option for consumers overwhelmed by credit card debt who want professional guidance without taking on new loans or risking debt settlement's credit damage. The main caveat is that its Debt Management Program requires consistent monthly payments over a multi-year period — it is a structured commitment, not a quick fix. The lead-capture form on the landing page requires a phone number and triggers SMS follow-up consent, which may feel intrusive for users just exploring options.
pros: BBB A+ rating displayed prominently on landing page
Free initial consultation with no obligation to enroll
Debt Management Program negotiates lower interest rates and monthly payments with creditors
Explicitly states 'ACCC does not loan money' — no predatory lending risk
Client portal (CreditU App + web login) for tracking program status and documents
Broad service range: debt management, bankruptcy counseling, reverse mortgage, homebuyer courses, student loan help
Toll-free phone access to live counselors (800-769-3571)
cons: Lead form requires phone number and triggers mandatory SMS consent before any information is provided
No fee information or program cost transparency visible on the landing page
Debt Management Program is a multi-year commitment — not a fast solution
Does not provide direct loans or emergency funds for consumers needing immediate cash
Services are enrollment-based; browsing anonymously for information is not easy
best_for: Consumers with $6K–$50K+ in credit card debt who are current or falling behind
People who want professional guidance but are wary of debt settlement's credit risks
Homeowners exploring reverse mortgages or first-time buyers needing HUD counseling
Individuals facing bankruptcy who need pre- or post-filing counseling
diagnosis: ACCC is best for consumers drowning in credit card debt who want a structured, non-profit-backed path to repayment without taking on new loans or pursuing debt settlement. The Debt Management Program is the flagship product and delivers real interest rate reductions, but it requires sustained monthly commitment over several years. Consumers seeking a quick fix, emergency cash, or anonymous information browsing will find the mandatory phone/SMS lead form a barrier.
services: Client portal and CreditU App for program tracking
